findings.title: Commercial setup converts stated and realized negotiation behavior into a reusable local practice profile

evidence.description: Requests signed agreements and an escalation matrix, extracts actual positions and deltas, and writes the resulting profile to the local plugin configuration.
Locates executed-contract repositories, requests templates and signed agreements, and defines template-to-signed-term delta and counterparty-pattern extraction.
Profile schema stores standard positions, acceptable fallbacks, hard prohibitions, carveouts, and evidence-derived adjustments.

source_to_sink: user-provided playbook, escalation matrix, templates, and signed agreements
skill reads and compares stated and signed positions
subsequent plugin skills read the profile
findings.proves: The design intentionally encodes institutional negotiation policy and empirically observed fallbacks in reusable local configuration.
findings.does_not_prove: It does not establish that the provider retains, pools, trains on, or productizes the profile or source documents.
findings.alternative_explanation: Customer-specific configuration is necessary to perform tailored contract review and is stored in a user-editable local file.
findings.test_to_confirm: Trace a configured deployment's file, model-request, connector, logging, retention, deletion, and training-control boundaries with packet/log evidence and contract terms.

findings.title: The interview explicitly elicits a seven-dimensional firm position on AI and model-data rights

evidence.description: Captures positions on training grants, privacy-policy incorporation, anonymization, competitive isolation, opt-outs, output ownership, and regulatory chain.

source_to_sink: AI/ML training-rights section of local practice profile
findings.proves: The repository can encode detailed organizational policy about vendor AI data use and competitive contamination.
findings.does_not_prove: The questions do not prove compliance with those positions by Anthropic or another vendor.
findings.alternative_explanation: This is a protective contract-review checklist designed to help customers resist unfavorable vendor terms.
findings.test_to_confirm: Inspect generated profiles and review outputs in a consented test tenant, then compare enforcement against executed vendor terms.

findings.title: Default matter isolation limits cross-matter reuse while archived matter content remains readable

evidence.description: States that cross-matter reads are disabled unless explicitly enabled and that archive is not deletion.
Defines default-off cross-matter context, requires explicit user request even when enabled, and leaves retention policy out of scope.

findings.proves: Prompt-level controls constrain cross-matter access by default, but closing a matter does not delete its content.
findings.does_not_prove: It does not demonstrate runtime enforcement, storage encryption, provider-side deletion, or retention duration.
findings.alternative_explanation: Readable archives support conflicts and retention obligations while explicit flags reduce accidental confidentiality crossover.
findings.test_to_confirm: Attempt adversarial cross-matter reads in a test tenant and inspect filesystem, audit, archive, and deletion behavior.

findings.title: Managed-agent deployment uploads custom skills and creates hosted agents

evidence.description: Deployment invokes skill uploads and POST /v1/agents for monitoring workflows over legal systems.
Documents inlining systems, uploading plugin skills, creating callable agents, and a one-level delegation limit.
Warns deployers to review Anthropic and local storage retention and says templates are not production-ready.

source_to_sink: hosted managed-agent execution configured against customer systems
findings.proves: Some prompt and skill definitions are intentionally uploaded to a hosted agent service, and deployed agents may process connected legal-system data.
findings.does_not_prove: It does not establish what customer content is retained, how long it is retained, or whether it is used for generalized training or cross-customer learning.
findings.alternative_explanation: Hosted artifacts are required to execute explicitly deployed monitoring agents for the customer.
findings.test_to_confirm: Capture deployment requests and agent runs, inventory transmitted fields, and obtain authenticated retention, training, support-access, and deletion terms.

findings.title: Managed-agent tiering is explicit counterevidence to unrestricted document-to-tool execution

evidence.description: Readers of untrusted documents have no MCP, Write, or network; analyzers receive bounded structured JSON; writers never see raw documents; orchestrator does not read raw material.

findings.proves: The documented reference architecture deliberately partitions untrusted inputs from network/write authority.
findings.does_not_prove: Documentation alone does not prove deployed manifests, runtime sandboxes, or connector implementations enforce every boundary.
findings.alternative_explanation: This is a substantive least-authority and prompt-injection control, not an exfiltration mechanism.
findings.test_to_confirm: Validate resolved manifests and run prompt-injection fixtures while observing actual tool invocations and denied calls.

findings.title: Legal Clinic intake captures highly sensitive facts but reserves acceptance and external action for human review

evidence.description: Defines structured client intake, conflicts and triage, while explicitly excluding case-acceptance decisions.
Default intake asks for immigration, criminal, family, safety, housing, and deadline facts.
States that external delivery requires a logged licensed-professional approval record.

source_to_sink: student analysis and professor review queue
findings.proves: The workflow handles privileged and highly sensitive client facts and includes explicit human responsibility gates.
findings.does_not_prove: It does not reveal transport, model-request content, tenant storage, provider logging, support access, or deletion behavior.
findings.alternative_explanation: The design reduces intake transcription burden while keeping legal acceptance and client/court delivery with humans.
findings.test_to_confirm: Use synthetic intake data to trace model, connector, storage, audit, approval, and deletion paths in a configured clinic tenant.

findings.title: Default MCP definitions introduce remote legal and workspace trust boundaries

evidence.description: Configures remote Slack, Google Drive, CourtListener, Courtroom5, and Descrybe MCP endpoints.
Requires HTTPS/auth, read-heavy tools, client confirmation for writes, provenance, and injection-resistance testing for default connectors.

source_to_sink: third-party connector operator and returned results
findings.proves: The plugin suite is designed to communicate with named remote connector operators and recognizes provenance and prompt-injection risks.
findings.does_not_prove: Configuration alone does not show which tools are authorized, what payloads are sent, whether a tenant enabled them, or provider/connector retention and reuse.
findings.alternative_explanation: Remote connectors provide requested authoritative research and document retrieval under customer authorization.
findings.test_to_confirm: Capture consented MCP request/response envelopes, OAuth scopes, authorization prompts, endpoint logs, and connector retention terms.

findings.title: Community-skill discovery is a supply-chain boundary with a restrictive default allowlist

evidence.description: Fetches watched registry indexes and caches skill frontmatter locally.
Previews full remote skills and adds registries only after confirmation.
Default policy fails closed for unknown sources and refuses undeclared connectors in restrictive mode.

source_to_sink: local registry cache and user preview
findings.proves: The ecosystem fetches executable-instruction candidates from external registries but ships a restrictive publisher/registry/connector allowlist and confirmation boundary.
findings.does_not_prove: It does not prove remote content integrity, immutable pinning, installation behavior, or that users keep restrictive mode enabled.
findings.alternative_explanation: The browser separates discovery from installation and uses explicit review plus fail-closed defaults to manage supply-chain risk.
findings.test_to_confirm: Test registry mutation, cache poisoning, publisher confusion, connector declaration, revision pinning, and installer confirmation behavior.

new_patterns.name: Realized-position extraction
new_patterns.logic: Find workflows that compare declared policy or templates with signed outcomes and promote the delta into reusable configuration.
new_patterns.false_positive_risk: May describe one-time document comparison without persistent policy update.

new_patterns.name: Hosted artifact deployment
new_patterns.logic: Trace local skill/agent definitions through upload and hosted-agent creation APIs separately from user-content flows.
new_patterns.false_positive_risk: Uploading prompt definitions does not itself imply uploading client documents or secondary use.

new_patterns.name: Archive-not-deletion boundary
new_patterns.logic: Find lifecycle actions labeled close/archive and determine whether content remains readable and where deletion/retention is governed.
search_terms: retention policy is out of scope
new_patterns.false_positive_risk: Retention may be legally required and customer-controlled rather than provider-controlled.

new_patterns.name: Instruction-candidate supply chain
new_patterns.logic: Trace external registries into cached or installed skill instructions and test allowlist, preview, confirmation, and revision binding.
new_patterns.false_positive_risk: Browsing and previewing content is distinct from executing or installing it.

negative_evidence.description: Lines 138-140 prohibit importing personal history, unrelated conversations, prior sessions, or ambient memory into the team profile without approval.
negative_evidence.effect: Weighs against ambient or covert profile construction in the documented setup path.

negative_evidence.description: Lines 173-177 make cross-matter reads default-off and require an explicit user request even when enabled.
negative_evidence.effect: Weighs against an unrestricted cross-matter pooling interpretation at the prompt-policy layer.

negative_evidence.description: Lines 39-45 separate untrusted readers from MCP/write authority and keep raw documents from writers and orchestrators.
negative_evidence.effect: Weighs against a designed direct prompt-injection-to-egress path.

negative_evidence.description: Lines 7-18 prefer read-heavy tools, require confirmation for writes, provenance, and injection-resistance checks.
negative_evidence.effect: Shows documented connector governance that is inconsistent with unconstrained egress by design.

negative_evidence.description: Lines 5-29 fail closed on unknown registries, publishers, and connector declarations.
negative_evidence.effect: Reduces the documented default supply-chain exposure, subject to runtime and user-configuration validation.

negative_evidence.description: Lines 97-102 prohibit advancing the sweep checkpoint before human acknowledgment.
negative_evidence.effect: Preserves human review and prevents silent suppression of unresolved governance gaps.

negative_evidence.description: Lines 81-85 prohibit silent low-confidence supplementation and preserve primary/secondary-source labels.
negative_evidence.effect: Weighs against autonomous laundering of uncertain sources into authoritative legal output.

unknowns.question: What exact customer content is included in hosted model, skill-upload, managed-agent, telemetry, support, and connector requests?
unknowns.why_source_cannot_answer: The repository contains prompts and deployment descriptions, not authenticated runtime request logs.
unknowns.needed_evidence: Consented packet captures, request logs, tenant audit exports, and field-level API documentation.

unknowns.question: How long do Anthropic and connector operators retain prompts, outputs, tool traces, uploaded skills, and managed-agent state?
unknowns.why_source_cannot_answer: The source warns users to review retention settings but does not encode authoritative service-side retention behavior.
unknowns.needed_evidence: Authenticated product settings, executed terms/DPA, subprocessors, deletion tests, and provider-side audit evidence.

unknowns.question: Are customer-specific profiles or behavioral traces used for generalized training, cross-customer learning, evaluations, product analytics, or support-derived product development?
unknowns.why_source_cannot_answer: No repository line supplies a retention-plus-secondary-use chain.
unknowns.needed_evidence: Contract terms, policy revisions, training-control attestations, data-lineage records, and independently reproducible source-to-sink evidence.

unknowns.question: Are matter-isolation, tool-tier, output-schema, and human-approval rules enforced by the deployed runtime?
unknowns.why_source_cannot_answer: The reviewed artifacts are declarative Markdown/YAML/JSON and cannot prove runtime enforcement.
unknowns.needed_evidence: Resolved deployment manifests, sandbox policy, adversarial execution logs, denied-tool events, and approval audit records.

unknowns.question: What OAuth scopes and payload fields are granted to each enabled MCP connector?
unknowns.why_source_cannot_answer: The .mcp.json files name endpoints and descriptions but not tenant grants or observed requests.
unknowns.needed_evidence: Tenant OAuth grants, connector schemas, request/response captures, operator logs, and retention terms.

unknowns.question: Does closing or deleting a local matter trigger deletion from model, connector, backup, support, and managed-agent systems?
unknowns.why_source_cannot_answer: The local prompt says archive is not deletion and leaves retention policy out of scope.
unknowns.needed_evidence: End-to-end deletion experiment with timestamps and confirmations from every processor and backup tier.
